revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Fallschirmtruppe (Kaiserlich Japanisches Heer)]][[ja:挺進連隊]][[uk:Повітрянодесантні війська Японії]]
[[File:IJA_Paratroopers_Teishin_Shudan_in_Palembang_Feb_1942.png|thumb|right|300px|Teishin soldiers during the Palembang Airborne Operation]] [[File:IJAAS-paratrooper-preparing-for-jump.jpg|thumb|200px|A Teishin soldier wearing specialized equipment]] '''Teishin Regiment''' (挺進連隊) refers to a type of {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|qid=Q276535|lt=unit formation of the Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}} ({{ill|Regiment|en|Regiment|qid=Q52371|lt=regiment|1=Regiment}}), specifically an airborne unit ({{ill|Parachute|en|Parachute|qid=Q482816|lt=paratrooper|1=Parachute}} {{ill|unit|ja|部隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/unit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Military unit|qid=Q176799}}) designed for {{ill|Airborne forces|en|Airborne forces|qid=Q864306|lt=airborne operations|1=Airborne forces}}. It was established during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}} and participated in operations such as the {{ill|Palembang Airborne Operation|ja|蘭印作戦#パレンバン空挺作戦|lt=Palembang drop operation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Palembang Airborne Operation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Palembang Airborne Operation|1=Dutch East Indies campaign|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q698226}}. It was commonly referred to as the '''Teishin Unit''' ('''陸軍挺進部隊'''), '''Imperial Japanese Army Paratrooper Unit''' ('''陸軍落下傘部隊'''), or '''Imperial Japanese Army Airborne Unit''' ('''陸軍空挺部隊'''). Its nickname was '''{{ill|The Paratroopers of the Sky|ja|空の神兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Paratroopers of the Sky|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Paratroopers of the Sky|1=Divine Soldiers of the Sky|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597341}}'''. The Teishin Regiment was part of a larger formation equivalent to a {{ill|Brigade|en|Brigade|qid=Q102356|lt=brigade|1=Brigade}}, known as the '''Teishin Brigade''' (挺進団), which incorporated multiple regiments and support units and was included in the {{ill|Order of battle|en|Order of battle|qid=Q11167066|lt=order of battle|1=Order of battle}}. In the later stages of the war, an even higher formation equivalent to a {{ill|Division (military)|en|Division (military)|qid=Q169534|lt=division|1=Division (military)}} was organized, known as the '''{{ill|Teishin Shudan|en|Teishin Shudan|qid=Q4101902|lt=1st Teishin Group|1=Teishin Shudan}}''' (挺進集団). In the Imperial Japanese Army, the military codes for these units were: * Teishin Regiment: '''Ri''' * Teishin Brigade: '''RB''' * Teishin Group: '''RD''' This article also details the '''Glider Infantry Regiment''' (滑空歩兵連隊, '''Ki'''), which was a glider-borne airborne unit. == Related Articles == * {{ill|The Paratroopers of the Sky|ja|空の神兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Paratroopers of the Sky|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Paratroopers of the Sky|1=Divine Soldiers of the Sky|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597341}} * {{ill|Japanese marine paratroopers of World War II|en|Japanese marine paratroopers of World War II|qid=Q4101904|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy Paratroopers|1=Japanese marine paratroopers of World War II}} - A paratrooper unit formed as part of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q219712}}'s {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy land forces|en|Imperial Japanese Navy land forces|qid=Q738438|lt=landing force|1=Imperial Japanese Navy land forces}}. * {{ill|1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)|en|1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)|qid=Q4596101|lt=1st Airborne Brigade|1=1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)}} - The airborne unit of the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|qid=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}}. * {{ill|Sunao Sonoda|en|Sunao Sonoda|qid=Q4388117|lt=Tadashi Sonoda|1=Sunao Sonoda}} == "Teishin" (挺進) == In the terminology of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Armed Forces|en|Imperial Japanese Armed Forces|qid=Q6139796|lt=Imperial Japanese military|1=Imperial Japanese Armed Forces}} (Army and Navy), the term "'''Teishin'''" (挺進) means "''to advance ahead, separated from the main force''" (proceeding into enemy territory further ahead of the front lines where the main force is located), and does not inherently imply airborne (airborne operations). Therefor== "Teishin" (挺進) == In the terminology of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Armed Forces|en|Imperial Japanese Armed Forces|qid=Q6139796|lt=Imperial Japanese military|1=Imperial Japanese Armed Forces}} (Army and Navy), the term "'''Teishin'''" (挺進) means "''to advance ahead, separated from the main force''" (proceeding into enemy territory further ahead of the front lines where the main force is located), and does not inherently imply airborne (airborne operations). Therefore, to specify airborne units, the term "'''Kūtei'''" (空挺), an abbreviation of "'''Kūchū Teishin'''” (空中挺進, ''airborne teishin'') or "'''Kūyu Teishin'''” (空輸挺進, ''air-transported teishin''), was coined by the Imperial Japanese Army. It is important to note that the use of the homophone "''Teishin''" (挺身)<ref>This term means "to sacrifice oneself" and was used in names such as {{ill|Teishin Slash Unit|ja|挺身斬込隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Slash Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Slash Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Women's Volunteer Corps|en|Women's Volunteer Corps|qid=Q11271215|lt=Women's Volunteer Corps|1=Women's Volunteer Corps}}.</ref> is a ''miswriting'' when referring to these airborne or commando units. The correct term is formally written as "'''挺進'''". Additionally, the Imperial Japanese Army incorporated units named "Teishin {{ill|Battalion|en|Battalion|qid=Q6382533|lt=Battalion|1=Battalion}}" into some ground forces such as divisions and brigades during the late stages of World War II. However, these were not airborne units but rather infantry with commando characteristics. For example: - The {{ill|Naganuma Teishin Unit|ja|永沼挺進隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naganuma Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naganuma Teishin Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}} was a cavalry commando unit. - The {{ill|Saeki Teishin Unit|ja|佐伯挺進隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Teishin Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Malayan campaign|en|Malayan campaign|qid=Q696664|lt=Malayan Campaign|1=Malayan campaign}} was an armored commando unit composed mainly of tanks and armored vehicles ({{ill|Reconnaissance regiments (Japan)|en|Reconnaissance regiments (Japan)|qid=Q11498133|lt=Reconnaissance Regiment|1=Reconnaissance regiments (Japan)}}). Furthermore, the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|ja|陸軍海上挺進戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|1=Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658693}}, formed in the late stages of the war, was a special attack unit operating {{ill|Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|ja|四式肉薄攻撃艇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|1=Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419877}} equipped with depth charges for close-range attacks against enemy ships. The {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|ja|海上挺進部隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|1=Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558687}} was a night raiding unit that conducted surprise attacks on U.S. supply convoys using {{ill|Kaiten|en|Kaiten|qid=Q277621|lt=Kaiten|1=Kaiten}} human torpedoes, launched from {{ill|Light cruiser|en|Light cruiser|qid=Q778129|lt=light cruisers|1=Light cruiser}} and {{ill|Destroyer|en|Destroyer|qid=Q174736|lt=destroyers|1=Destroyer}}. e, to specify airborne units, the term "'''Kūtei'''" (空挺), an abbreviation of "'''Kūchū Teishin'''” (空中挺進, ''airborne teishin'') or "'''Kūyu Teishin'''” (空輸挺進, ''air-transported teishin''), was coined by the Imperial Japanese Army. It is important to note that the use of the homophone "''Teishin''" (挺身)<ref>This term means "to sacrifice oneself" and was used in names such as {{ill|Teishin Slash Unit|ja|挺身斬込隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Slash Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Slash Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Women's Volunteer Corps|en|Women's Volunteer Corps|qid=Q11271215|lt=Women's Volunteer Corps|1=Women's Volunteer Corps}}.</ref> is a ''miswriting'' when referring to these airborne or commando units. The correct term is formally written as "'''挺進'''". Additionally, the Imperial Japanese Army incorporated units named "Teishin {{ill|Battalion|en|Battalion|qid=Q6382533|lt=Battalion|1=Battalion}}" into some ground forces such as divisions and brigades during the late stages of World War II. However, these were not airborne units but rather infantry with commando characteristics. For example: - The {{ill|Naganuma Teishin Unit|ja|永沼挺進隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naganuma Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naganuma Teishin Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}} was a cavalry commando unit. - The {{ill|Saeki Teishin Unit|ja|佐伯挺進隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Teishin Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Malayan campaign|en|Malayan campaign|qid=Q696664|lt=Malayan Campaign|1=Malayan campaign}} was an armored commando unit composed mainly of tanks and armored vehicles ({{ill|Reconnaissance regiments (Japan)|en|Reconnaissance regiments (Japan)|qid=Q11498133|lt=Reconnaissance Regiment|1=Reconnaissance regiments (Japan)}}). Furthermore, the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|ja|陸軍海上挺進戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|1=Imperial Japanese Army Naval Teishin Squadron|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658693}}, formed in the late stages of the war, was a special attack unit operating {{ill|Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|ja|四式肉薄攻撃艇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|1=Type 4 Shallow-Water Assault Boats|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419877}} equipped with depth charges for close-range attacks against enemy ships. The {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|ja|海上挺進部隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|1=Imperial Japanese Navy Teishin Unit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558687}} was a night raiding unit that conducted surprise attacks on U.S. supply convoys using {{ill|Kaiten|en|Kaiten|qid=Q277621|lt=Kaiten|1=Kaiten}} human torpedoes, launched from {{ill|Light cruiser|en|Light cruiser|qid=Q778129|lt=light cruisers|1=Light cruiser}} and {{ill|Destroyer|en|Destroyer|qid=Q174736|lt=destroyers|1=Destroyer}}. == Unit Insignia == [[File:IJA-Para-Insignia.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Unit Insignia]] The {{ill|Teishin Unit|ja|挺進部隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Unit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Unit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} used a unit insignia (unit emblem) designed with a stylized parachute ('''circle with a diagonal cross'''). {{ill|Paratrooper|en|Paratrooper|qid=Q749387|lt=Teishin Soldier|1=Paratrooper}} (paratroopers) wore this unit insignia as a badge on their {{ill|Uniforms of the Imperial Japanese Army|en|Uniforms of the Imperial Japanese Army|qid=Q4121119|lt=Imperial Japanese Army uniform|1=Uniforms of the Imperial Japanese Army}}. Additionally, this design was also used as the unit insignia for Teishin Flying Squadrons and was depicted on the vertical stabilizers of their assigned transport aircraft.<ref>In addition to this, official unit insignias were designated for other Imperial Japanese Army flying units and tank units. Examples of designs include the "lightning bolt" for the and {{ill|50th Flying Regiment|ja|飛行第50戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/50th Flying Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/50th Flying Regiment|1=50th Flying Regiment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665849}}, the "diagonal arrow" for the {{ill|64th Flying Regiment|ja|飛行第64戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/64th Flying Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/64th Flying Regiment|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=加藤隼戦闘隊|1=Katō Hayabusa Sentōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q114797111}}, the character "士" for the {{ill|11th Tank Regiment|ja|戦車第11連隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11th Tank Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11th Tank Regiment|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the "circle with an arrow" for the {{ill|26th Tank Regiment|ja|戦車第26連隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26th Tank Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26th Tank Regiment|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qid=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} (Reiwa 2), the {{ill|Association for the Maintenance of the Sky Divine Soldier Memorial|ja|空の神兵慰霊顕彰碑護持会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association for the Maintenance of the Sky Divine Soldier Memorial|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association for the Maintenance of the Sky Divine Soldier Memorial|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a general incorporated association, applied to the {{ill|Japan Patent Office|en|Japan Patent Office|qid=Q1141991|lt=Japan Patent Office|1=Japan Patent Office}} to register this unit insignia as a trademark, and it was officially recognized.</ref> Furthermore, in {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), a "'''Special Chest Badge for Teishin Unit Paratroopers'''" (commonly known as the "Teishin Chest Badge") was established, featuring a golden {{ill|Black kite|en|Black kite|qid=Q80362|lt=kite|1=Black kite}} with outstretched wings ('''{{ill|Golden Kite|ja|金鵄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Golden Kite|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Golden Kite|1=Golden Kite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11648028}}'''). This badge was designated for Teishin Unit commanders and paratroopers belonging to Teishin Units, who wore it on the right side of their military uniforms.<ref>In addition to this, the Imperial Japanese Army had other chest badges such as the "Aviation Chest Badge" for those related to aviation units and schools, the "{{ill|Special Aviation Chest Badge for Aircrew|ja|軍服 (大日本帝国陸軍)#空中勤務者胸章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Aviation Chest Badge for Aircrew|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Aviation Chest Badge for Aircrew|1=Uniforms of the Imperial Japanese Army|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4121119}}" (Aircrew Badge) for aircrew members, and the "{{ill|Naval Chest Badge|ja|軍服 (大日本帝国陸軍)#船舶胸章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naval Chest Badge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naval Chest Badge|1=Uniforms of the Imperial Japanese Army|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4121119}}" for {{ill|Army Naval Personnel|ja|陸軍船舶兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Naval Personnel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Army Naval Personnel|1=Army Naval Personnel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658709}}.</ref> == Overview == === Establishment === Inspired by the achievements of the {{ill|Fallschirmjäger|en|Fallschirmjäger|qid=Q13479665|lt=German paratroopers|1=Fallschirmjäger}} in the early stages of World War II, the Imperial Japanese Army began developing airborne units and paratroopers (Teishin soldiers). Research started in the autumn of 1940, using photos of the {{ill|United States Army|en|United States Army|qid=Q9212|lt=United States Army|1=United States Army}} airborne units provided by {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}. The {{ill|Hamamatsu Army Flight School|ja|浜松陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu Army Flight School|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu Army Flight School|1=Hamamatsu Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557890}} established a "training section," gradually organizing equipment and personnel. After parachute training at {{ill|Yomiuri Amusement Park|ja|二子玉川園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomiuri Amusement Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomiuri Amusement Park|1=Futako Tamagawa-en|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11371458}}, the first successful manned jump occurred on February 20, 1941.<ref>Yomiuri Amusement Park's parachute tower continued to be used for basic training until around 1942, with the {{ill|Yomiuri Shimbun|en|Yomiuri Shimbun|qid=Q645218|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}} receiving a certificate of appreciation from the army.</ref> Later, the unit moved to the {{ill|Bai Chengzi Army Flight School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bai Chengzi Army Flight School|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bai Chengzi Army Flight School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11008142}} in Manchuria in May, returning to {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} ({{ill|Nyutabaru|ja|新田原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyutabaru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyutabaru|qq=}} and {{ill|Karasuhara|ja|唐瀬原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasuhara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasuhara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in October to become the "Army Teishin Training Unit."<ref>Kenji Doihara, "Report on the Organization of the Army Teishin Training Unit," JACAR Ref.C04123718200</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|author=Association to Honor the Spirits of the War Dead|year=1999|date=1999-3-31|title=Veterans Speak Out|url=http://www.eireinikotaerukai.com/pdf/tayori/Eireitayori_No34.pdf|journal=Newsletter to Honor the Spirits|volume=34|qq=}}</ref> Colonel {{ill|Seiichi Kume|ja|久米精一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiichi Kume|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiichi Kume|1=Seiichi Kume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60988904}} was appointed as the head of the training unit. On November 5, the first airborne unit, "Instructional Teishin 1st Regiment," was formed. === First Battle === [[File:IJA-paratroopers-Palembang-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Teishin forces parachuting over Palembang]] Just before the outbreak of the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} in December 1941, the "1st Teishin Regiment" (1Ri)<ref>Personnel and equipment from the Instructional Teishin 1st Regiment were transferred.</ref> and the "Teishin Flight Squadron" (RFR), responsible for airborne transport, formed the "1st Teishin Brigade" (1RB).<ref>Kenji Doihara, "Report on the Formation of the 1st Teishin Brigade," JACAR Ref.C04123676400</ref> Soon after, the "2nd Teishin Regiment" (2Ri) was also organized. During the {{ill|Dutch East Indies campaign|en|Dutch East Indies campaign|qid=Q698226|lt=Dutch East Indies campaign|1=Dutch East Indies campaign}}, the 1st Teishin Brigade, led by Colonel Kume, successfully carried out Japan's first airborne operation over {{ill|Palembang|en|Palembang|qid=Q8131|lt=Palembang|1=Palembang}}, despite missing the 1st Teishin Regiment.<ref>Japan's first airborne operation was actually conducted about a month earlier by the Navy during the Menado operation.</ref> === Expansion === [[File:IJA-para-helmet.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Teishin soldier]] After the {{ill|Southern Expeditionary Campaign|ja|南方作戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Expeditionary Campaign|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Expeditionary Campaign|1=Southern Operations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10907971}}, the 1st Teishin Brigade demonstrated a parachute exercise, "Operation Te," before Emperor {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|qid=Q34479|lt=Shōwa|1=Hirohito}} in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}. Subsequently, the brigade headquarters was demobilized and restructured as the Army Teishin Training Unit, with each regiment returning under its command. During this period, the potential of {{ill|Military glider|en|Military glider|qid=Q601924|lt=military gliders|1=Military glider}} became evident, leading to the formation of the 5th Teishin Regiment, later renamed the "Glider Infantry Regiment" (Ki). Eventually, four Teishin Regiments and two Glider Infantry Regiments became core forces. Research into {{ill|airborne tanks|ja|空挺戦車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/airborne tanks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/airborne tanks|1=airborne tank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597419}} also progressed, resulting in the formation of the "Teishin Tank Unit" (RTK). By June 1943, the 1st Teishin Brigade advanced to {{ill|Peleliu|ja|ペリリュー島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peleliu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peleliu|1=Peleliu Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q14752295}} and {{ill|Wewak|en|Wewak|qid=Q1017763|lt=Wewak|1=Wewak}}, though planned operations in {{ill|Benabena|ja|ベナベナ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benabena|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benabena|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524602|1=Benabena, Papua New Guinea}} (inland New Guinea) were canceled. In November, they moved to {{ill|Sumatra|en|Sumatra|qid=Q3492|lt=Sumatra|1=Sumatra}} for parachute training with the {{ill|Indian National Army|en|Indian National Army|qid=Q156062|lt=Indian National Army|1=Indian National Army}}, before returning to Japan in July 1944.<ref>Trained INA soldiers conducted drops in {{ill|Akyab|ja|シットウェー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akyab|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akyab|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シットウェ|1=Sittwe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q738748}}, western Burma. Tanaka (1976), pp. 220–221.</ref> === Teishin Group === With the start of the {{ill|Battle of Leyte|en|Battle of Leyte|qid=Q717004|lt=Battle of Leyte|1=Battle of Leyte}} in autumn 1944, the "1st Teishin Group" (1RD), effectively an "airborne division," was formed in late November, integrating all army airborne units.<ref>Tanaka (1976), pp. 198–200.</ref> However, the 2nd Teishin Brigade had already advanced to {{ill|Luzon|en|Luzon|qid=Q125384|lt=Luzon|1=Luzon}}, and coordination under a unified command proved challenging. === Late War Battles === [[File:Giretsu Kukeitai - parade before departure.jpg|thumb|right|200px|{{ill|Giretsu Kuteitai|en|Giretsu Kuteitai|qid=Q5564232|lt=Giretsu Airborne Unit|1=Giretsu Kuteitai}}]] During the war's later stages, airborne units targeted enemy {{ill|Aerodrome|en|Aerodrome|qid=Q62447|lt=airfields|1=Aerodrome}} to destroy aircraft on the ground. In the Battle of Leyte, the "2nd Teishin Brigade" (2RB) conducted successful operations as the "Takachiho Airborne Unit." In the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|qid=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa|1=Battle of Okinawa}}, the "{{ill|Giretsu Kuteitai|en|Giretsu Kuteitai|qid=Q5564232|lt=Giretsu Airborne Unit|1=Giretsu Kuteitai}}" carried out commando raids on American-occupied airfields. Other planned operations, such as "Operation Ken" targeting airfields and {{ill|atomic bomb|ja|原爆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/atomic bomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/atomic bomb|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=原子爆弾|1=Atomic bomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q650051}} storage in the {{ill|Mariana Islands|en|Mariana Islands|qid=Q153732|lt=Mariana Islands|1=Mariana Islands}}, were canceled with Japan's surrender. === Postwar === After Japan's defeat, the army and Teishin units were disbanded. However, in 1954, the newly established {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|qid=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}} began developing airborne units. Twenty former Teishin soldiers, led by {{ill|Hayao Kinugasa|en|Hayao Kinugasa|qid=Q7477342|lt=Shun’o Kinugasa|1=Hayao Kinugasa}}, underwent parachute training with the U.S. Army. In 1955, the "Provisional Airborne Training Unit" was formed, evolving into the "1st Airborne Brigade" in 1958. Kinugasa, as the founder of JGSDF airborne forces, held key leadership roles, including the first brigade commander. The traditions of the Teishin forces were inherited by the 1st Airborne Brigade, with strong cultural ties evident in commemorations, such as the "Monument to the Divine Soldiers of the Sky" at {{ill|Narashino Garrison|ja|習志野駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashino Garrison|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashino Garrison|1=Camp Narashino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610513}}. == Organization == [[File:IJA-paratroopers-Palembang-4.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Teishin soldiers retrieving various weapons and equipment from "supply containers" dropped separately by parachute. A {{ill|Type 99 light machine gun|en|Type 99 light machine gun|qid=Q696792|lt=Type 99 light machine gun|1=Type 99 light machine gun}} can be seen in the background on the left.]] [[File:IJA-paratroopers-Palembang-8.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Teishin soldier picking up a {{ill|Type 100 flamethrower|ja|一〇〇式火焔発射機|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 100 flamethrower|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 100 flamethrower|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=九三式/一〇〇式火焔発射機|1=Type 93/Type 100 flamethrower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3486997}} after landing.]] === Early War Teishin Regiment === Organization of the 1st Teishin Regiment as of November 1941. Total personnel: 773.<ref>Kenji Doihara, "Report on Submission of Documents Regarding the Provisional Formation of the 1st Teishin Brigade," JACAR Ref.C04123676400</ref> Unlike an {{ill|infantry regiment|ja|歩兵連隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/infantry regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/infantry regiment|1=infantry regiment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10358588}}, it was a smaller regiment without battalion-level organization, and the regimental commander held the Rank of {{ill|Major (rank)|en|Major (rank)|qid=Q983927|lt=Major|1=Major (rank)}}. Functionally similar to an infantry battalion, it lacked dedicated machine gun companies, with machine guns distributed directly to the companies from the start. * Regimental Headquarters (60 personnel excluding the Signal Section) ** Signal Section (45 personnel) * Companies (167 personnel each) ×4 ** Platoons ×3 ** Machine Gun Platoon === Late War Teishin Regiment === Organization of the 3rd and 4th Teishin Regiments after reorganization in September 1943. While the absence of battalion-level organization remained unchanged, a heavy weapons company was added to increase firepower. Total personnel: 816. Notably, the 5th Teishin Regiment at the time of its formation consisted of one infantry battalion of similar size and a heavy weapons battalion. This included temporarily attached {{ill|Mountain gun|en|Mountain gun|qid=Q1497061|lt=mountain artillery|1=Mountain gun}} companies, {{ill|Quick-firing gun|en|Quick-firing gun|qid=Q383028|lt=rapid-fire gun|1=Quick-firing gun}} companies, and {{ill|Autocannon|en|Autocannon|qid=Q751705|lt=machine gun|1=Autocannon}} companies, which were intended to be directly under the command of the {{ill|Teishin Group|ja|挺進集団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=第1挺進集団|1=Teishin Shudan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4101902}}.<ref>Tanaka (1976), p. 189.</ref> The regimental commander was either a {{ill|Lieutenant colonel|en|Lieutenant colonel|qid=Q493898|lt=Lieutenant Colonel|1=Lieutenant colonel}} or Major. * Regimental Headquarters * Companies (158 personnel each) ×3 * Engineer Company (158 personnel) - Infantry with combined {{ill|combat engineer|ja|工兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/combat engineer|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/combat engineer|1=military engineer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134384333}} functions, equipped with equipment for destroying {{ill|Wire obstacle|en|Wire obstacle|qid=Q91451|lt=barbed wire|1=Wire obstacle}} and six {{ill|Flamethrower|en|Flamethrower|qid=Q45183|lt=flamethrowers|1=Flamethrower}}.<ref name="syogo409">{{ill|Japan Ministry of Defense National Institute for Defense Studies|ja|防衛研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Ministry of Defense National Institute for Defense Studies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Ministry of Defense National Institute for Defense Studies|1=National Institute for Defense Studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657111}} War History Office, "Philippines Sho-Go Army Aviation Operations," {{ill|Asagumo Shimbunsha|ja|朝雲新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asagumo Shimbunsha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asagumo Shimbunsha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=朝雲新聞|1=Asagumo Shinbunsha. Henshūkyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11517751}} ({{ill|Senshi Sōsho|en|Senshi Sōsho|qid=Q710061|lt=Senshi Sōsho|1=Senshi Sōsho}}), 1971, p. 409.</ref> * Heavy Weapons Company (148 personnel) - Equipped with four {{ill|Type 92 battalion gun|en|Type 92 battalion gun|qid=Q706233|lt=Type 92 battalion guns|1=Type 92 battalion gun}} and four {{ill|Type 97 81 mm infantry mortar|en|Type 97 81 mm infantry mortar|qid=Q1014037|lt=Type 97 infantry mortars|1=Type 97 81 mm infantry mortar}}.<ref name="syogo409" /> === Glider Infantry Regiment === The Glider Infantry Regiment, like the Teishin Regiment, was a small regiment without battalion-level organization. Although similar in structure, it was equipped with relatively long-range heavy weapons due to landing via gliders rather than parachute drops. Total personnel numbered in the eight hundreds.<ref>Japan Ministry of Defense National Institute for Defense Studies War History Office, "Army Aviation Equipment and Operations (3) Until the End of the War," Asagumo Shimbunsha (Senshi Sōsho), 1976, p. 271.</ref> * Regimental Headquarters * Companies ×3 * Engineer Company * Mountain Artillery Company - Equipped with four {{ill|Type 94 75 mm mountain gun|en|Type 94 75 mm mountain gun|qid=Q6133787|lt=Type 94 mountain guns|1=Type 94 75 mm mountain gun}} * Rapid-Fire Gun Company - Equipped with six {{ill|Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun|en|Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun|qid=Q93533|lt=Type 1 47 mm anti-tank guns|1=Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun}} == Military History == === Palembang Airborne Operation === [[File:IJA-1st-Yokosuka-Force-Paratroopers-Manado-Langoan-Airfield.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teishin troops descending on Palembang]] {{seealso|Dutch East Indies Campaign#Palembang Airborne Operation|qq=}} In 1942, the Teishin 1st Regiment was scheduled to participate in the Palembang Airborne Operation, but due to a fire accident on the transport ship "Meiko Maru" which resulted in its sinking and the loss of equipment, coupled with an outbreak of paratyphoid within the unit, the Teishin 2nd Regiment replaced it. On February 14, supported by the Teishin Air Squadron, the 12th Transport Flying Company, {{ill|98th Flying Regiment|ja|飛行第98戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/98th Flying Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/98th Flying Regiment|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (responsible for supply drops), {{ill|64th Flying Regiment|ja|飛行第64戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/64th Flying Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/64th Flying Regiment|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=加藤隼戦闘隊|1=Katō Hayabusa Sentōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q114797111}} and {{ill|59th Flying Regiment|ja|飛行第59戦隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/59th Flying Regiment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/59th Flying Regiment|1=59th Flying Regiment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q795535}} (providing fighter cover, led by the 64th Regiment's commander {{ill|Tateo Katō|en|Tateo Katō|qid=Q7688005|lt=Kato Tateo|1=Tateo Katō}}), approximately 400 men from the 1st Teishin Brigade (formed from the 2nd Regiment) parachuted and engaged in combat with Dutch forces. As a result, they secured the vital Palembang oil fields, Royal Dutch Shell’s refineries, and two airfields nearly unscathed. The capture of Palembang, one of East Asia's most important oil-producing regions, was critical to Japan's strategic objectives in the Pacific War. The Teishin Regiment's remarkable performance was announced the following day in the Imperial General Headquarters' report, No. 192. {{quotation| "Imperial General Headquarters Announcement, February 15, 5:10 PM. The powerful Imperial Army paratrooper unit successfully executed a surprise drop on Palembang, Sumatra Island’s largest oil field, at 11:26 AM on February 14. They defeated the enemy, securing airfields and other key locations while expanding their gains. Army aviation units closely supported the operation, and part of the unit advanced to the airfield on the 15th. End."|qq=}} This achievement was widely publicized through mass media and reinforced by films, military songs, and artwork, establishing the Teishin Regiment's reputation as "Sky Soldiers" and "Imperial Army Paratroopers." === Lashio Airborne Operation === In March 1942, following the conclusion of the Java Campaign, the Teishin Brigade was ordered to advance to Burma. The participating units included the Teishin 1st Regiment, the 3rd Company of the 2nd Regiment, and an assembled unit under the command of Major Takeda, the 1st Regiment commander. They departed Phnom Penh by train on March 19 and arrived in Rangoon on April 8. Their mission was to encircle retreating enemy forces by parachuting into Lashio to coordinate with the advancing {{ill|56th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|en|56th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|qid=Q4640737|lt=56th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|1=56th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)}}. Due to delays in assembling the air squadrons and concerns about committing under-strength forces, the operation faced postponements. Although the 5th Flying Division planned the assault for May 5, rapid advances by the 56th Division forced the operation to be rescheduled for April 29. However, bad weather caused the mission to be aborted mid-flight, resulting in crashes during the return. The 56th Division captured Lashio by noon on the same day. === Takachiho Airborne Unit === [[File:IJA-paras at Clark Field, Luzon 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Teishin soldiers preparing for deployment at Clark Airfield, Luzon]] {{seealso|Battle of Leyte#Airfield Recapture Operations|qq=}} During the Battle of Leyte, the 2nd Teishin Brigade (code-named "Takachiho Unit") conducted airborne assaults on the Burauen airfields, Tacloban airfield, and Dulag airfield. Named "Operation Te," the objective was to temporarily secure these airfields to indirectly support Japanese maritime convoys. On December 6, 1944, the Teishin 3rd Regiment and part of the 4th Regiment launched from Luzon, transported by the 1st Teishin Air Squadron (code-named "Kirishima Unit") and elements of the 2nd Squadron. Approximately 460 paratroopers participated, with support from 35 transport aircraft, 4 heavy bombers, and over 30 fighter planes. The Takachiho Unit engaged in intense battles with the American {{ill|11th Airborne Division|en|11th Airborne Division|qid=Q169980|lt=11th Airborne Division (United States)|1=11th Airborne Division}}, achieving limited success before operations were suspended due to failed ground offensives and maritime transports. === Clark Airfield Defense === {{seealso|1st Teishin Group|Battle of Luzon#Battle for the Clark Area|qq=}} In the Battle of Luzon, the 1st Teishin Group, primarily composed of the 2nd Glider Infantry Regiment, defended the Clark Airfield area. Due to diminishing prospects for large-scale airborne operations, most transport aircraft were left behind, and many Teishin units remained in Japan. Some units en route, like the 1st Glider Infantry Regiment, were lost when the carrier "Unryu" was sunk. Despite fierce resistance, the Japanese defense collapsed under the superior American forces. The remnants retreated into the mountains, fighting until Japan's surrender. === Giretsu Airborne Unit === [[File:Giretsu - Michiro Okuyama & Chuichi Suwabe.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Captain Okuyama and Captain Suwabe of the Giretsu Airborne Unit shaking hands before departure]] {{main|Operation Gi|qq=}} Formed from elite members of the Teishin 1st Regiment, the Giretsu Airborne Unit launched a daring attack on American-held airfields in Okinawa using 12 {{ill|Mitsubishi Ki-21|en|Mitsubishi Ki-21|qid=Q1253358|lt=Mitsubishi Ki-21|1=Mitsubishi Ki-21}}. At least one plane successfully landed, inflicting damage on American aircraft and facilities. == Equipment == [[File:IJAAS-paratrooper-with-flamethrower.jpg|thumb|250px|Teishin soldiers equipped and deployed after the drop. They are holding the {{ill|Type 100 flamethrower|ja|一〇〇式火焔発射機|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 100 flamethrower|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 100 flamethrower|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=九三式/一〇〇式火焔発射機|1=Type 93/Type 100 flamethrower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3486997}} (foreground) and the {{ill|Type 99 light machine gun|en|Type 99 light machine gun|qid=Q696792|lt=Type 99 light machine gun|1=Type 99 light machine gun}} (center)]] [[File:IJA Fallschirmjäger beim Sprung mit Typ 1 Fallschirm.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teishin soldier descending with the {{ill|Type 1 parachute|ja|一式落下傘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 1 parachute|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 1 parachute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] [[File:IJA Typ 4 Fallschirm.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teishin soldier equipped with the {{ill|Type 4 parachute|ja|四式落下傘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 parachute|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 parachute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] [[File:IJA-paras at Clark Field, Luzon 2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teishin soldiers boarding the {{ill|Kawasaki Ki-56|en|Kawasaki Ki-56|qid=Q209940|lt=Type 1 cargo transport aircraft|1=Kawasaki Ki-56}}]] [[File:Absprung mit Typ 1 Spezial.jpg|thumb|right|200px|{{ill|Nakajima Ki-34|en|Nakajima Ki-34|qid=Q1991281|lt=Type 97 transport aircraft|1=Nakajima Ki-34}} used for parachute training]] [[File:IJA-paratroopers-Palembang-7.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teishin soldiers inside the transport aircraft]] [[File:IJA-paratroopers-Palembang-11.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teishin troops descending]] === Weapons === * {{ill|Type 94 Nambu pistol|en|Type 94 Nambu pistol|qid=Q194133|lt=Type 94 pistol|1=Type 94 Nambu pistol}} - Issued to all personnel as a sidearm. * {{ill|Type 99 rifle|en|Type 99 rifle|qid=Q663647|lt=Type 99 short rifle|1=Type 99 rifle}} * {{ill|TERA rifle|en|TERA rifle|qid=Q2368879|lt=Type 2 rifle|1=TERA rifle}} (Tera rifle) - A disassemblable rifle for parachute drops, used in the latter stages of the war. * {{ill|Type 99 light machine gun|en|Type 99 light machine gun|qid=Q696792|lt=Type 99 light machine gun|1=Type 99 light machine gun}} - Main light machine gun. * {{ill|Type 92 heavy machine gun|en|Type 92 heavy machine gun|qid=Q597743|lt=Type 92 heavy machine gun|1=Type 92 heavy machine gun}} - Main heavy machine gun. * {{ill|Type 100 submachine gun|en|Type 100 submachine gun|qid=Q1141411|lt=Type 100 submachine gun|1=Type 100 submachine gun}} - Used by Takachiho Airborne Unit and Giretsu Airborne Unit. * {{ill|Thompson submachine gun|en|Thompson submachine gun|qid=Q269593|lt=Thompson submachine gun|1=Thompson submachine gun}} - 600 units supplied after capture during the {{ill|Fall of Singapore|en|Fall of Singapore|qid=Q296754|lt=Battle of Singapore|1=Fall of Singapore}}.<ref>Tanaka (1976), pp. 130–131.</ref> * {{ill|Type 97 automatic cannon|en|Type 97 automatic cannon|qid=Q615172|lt=Type 97 automatic cannon|1=Type 97 automatic cannon}} * {{ill|Type 97 grenade|en|Type 97 grenade|qid=Q2447937|lt=Type 97 hand grenade|1=Type 97 grenade}} * {{ill|Type 99 grenade|en|Type 99 grenade|qid=Q3116432|lt=Type 99 hand grenade|1=Type 99 grenade}} * {{ill|Type 100 grenade discharger|en|Type 100 grenade discharger|qid=Q7860665|lt=Type 100 grenade discharger|1=Type 100 grenade discharger}} * {{ill|Type 100 flamethrower|ja|一〇〇式火焔発射機|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 100 flamethrower|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 100 flamethrower|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=九三式/一〇〇式火焔発射機|1=Type 93/Type 100 flamethrower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3486997}} * {{ill|Type 89 grenade discharger|en|Type 89 grenade discharger|qid=Q194129|lt=Type 89 grenade discharger|1=Type 89 grenade discharger}} * {{ill|Type 30 bayonet|en|Type 30 bayonet|qid=Q4457926|lt=Type 30 bayonet|1=Type 30 bayonet}} * {{ill|Type 2 bayonet|ja|二式銃剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 2 bayonet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 2 bayonet|1=Type 2 bayonet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11371557}} === Armored Vehicles and Artillery === * {{ill|Type 94 37 mm anti-tank gun|en|Type 94 37 mm anti-tank gun|qid=Q7860822|lt=Type 94 37 mm anti-tank gun|1=Type 94 37 mm anti-tank gun}} - Used as an infantry gun. * {{ill|Type 92 battalion gun|en|Type 92 battalion gun|qid=Q706233|lt=Type 92 battalion gun|1=Type 92 battalion gun}} * {{ill|Type 97 81 mm infantry mortar|en|Type 97 81 mm infantry mortar|qid=Q1014037|lt=Type 97 infantry mortar|1=Type 97 81 mm infantry mortar}} * {{ill|Type 94 75 mm mountain gun|en|Type 94 75 mm mountain gun|qid=Q6133787|lt=Type 94 mountain gun|1=Type 94 75 mm mountain gun}} * {{ill|Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun|en|Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun|qid=Q93533|lt=Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun|1=Type 1 47 mm anti-tank gun}} * {{ill|Type 95 Ha-Go light tank|en|Type 95 Ha-Go light tank|qid=Q696563|lt=Type 95 Ha-Go|1=Type 95 Ha-Go light tank}} - Used until the deployment of the Type 2 light tank. * {{ill|Type 2 Ke-To light tank|en|Type 2 Ke-To light tank|qid=Q701360|lt=Type 2 Ke-To|1=Type 2 Ke-To light tank}} - Airborne tank for glider deployment, assigned to the Teishin Tank Unit. * {{ill|Special number 3 light tank Ku-Ro|en|Special number 3 light tank Ku-Ro|qid=Q11570717|lt=Special Type 3 tank|1=Special number 3 light tank Ku-Ro}} - Planned airborne tank capable of glider transport, remained at the conceptual stage. * {{ill|Kurogane Type 95|en|Kurogane Type 95|qid=Q1785885|lt=Type 95 Kurogane|1=Kurogane Type 95}} - Light vehicle designed for glider transport, later modified for combat with machine gun mounts.<ref>[http://ksa.axisz.jp/G-12HikoDaiichisentai.htm Phantom "Glider Flight First Squadron"]</ref> === Parachutes === * {{ill|Type 1 parachute|ja|一式落下傘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 1 parachute|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 1 parachute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Type 4 parachute|ja|四式落下傘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 parachute|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Type 4 parachute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Used by the Takachiho Airborne Unit. === Transport Aircraft === * {{ill|Mitsubishi Ki-57|en|Mitsubishi Ki-57|qid=Q332039|lt=Type 100 transport aircraft|1=Mitsubishi Ki-57}} - Main transport aircraft, used in the Palembang Airborne Operation and by the Takachiho Airborne Unit. * {{ill|Kawasaki Ki-56|en|Kawasaki Ki-56|qid=Q209940|lt=Type 1 cargo transport aircraft|1=Kawasaki Ki-56}} - Secondary transport aircraft. * {{ill|Mitsubishi Ki-21|en|Mitsubishi Ki-21|qid=Q1253358|lt=Type 97 heavy bomber|1=Mitsubishi Ki-21}} - Used for supply drops and forced landings. * {{ill|Nakajima Ki-49|en|Nakajima Ki-49|qid=Q1393091|lt=Type 100 heavy bomber|1=Nakajima Ki-49}} - Four units used for forced landings by the Takachiho Airborne Unit. * {{ill|Nakajima Ki-34|en|Nakajima Ki-34|qid=Q1991281|lt=Type 97 transport aircraft|1=Nakajima Ki-34}} - Used for training by the Instructional Teishin Regiment and Flight Squadron. * {{ill|Tachikawa Ki-54|en|Tachikawa Ki-54|qid=Q739725|lt=Type 1 twin-engine advanced trainer|1=Tachikawa Ki-54}} - Transport version used for parachute training. * {{ill|Ku-1|ja|ク-1 (航空機)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ku-1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ku-1|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=二式小型輸送滑空機|1=Maeda Ku-1|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3271004}} - First operational transport glider, capacity of 8 including crew, mainly used for training. * {{ill|Ku-7|ja|ク-7 (航空機)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ku-7|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ku-7|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ク7 (航空機)|1=Kokusai Ku-7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q609106}} - Prototype large glider; its powered variant was the {{ill|Ki-105|ja|キ105 (航空機)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-105|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-105|1=Kokusai Ki-105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6148348}} prototype transport aircraft. * {{ill|Ku-8|ja|ク-8 (航空機)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ku-8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ku-8|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=四式特殊輸送機|1=Kokusai Ku-8|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q583548}} - Main glider, with the mass-produced version designated Ku-8-II. == List of Teishin Regiments == {{section-stub|qq=}} * '''1st Teishin Regiment''' ('''1Ri''') - Reorganized from the Instructional Teishin 1st Regiment. At the end of the war, the main force was stationed in Yokoshiba, with the Sonoda Unit in Chitose. * '''2nd Teishin Regiment''' ('''2Ri''') - Stationed in {{ill|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|en|Kawaminami, Miyazaki|qid=Q964194|lt=Kawanami, Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Kawaminami, Miyazaki}} at the end of the war. * '''3rd Teishin Regiment''' ('''3Ri''') - Known as the Instructional Teishin 3rd Regiment at certain times. Its code name during the Leyte Operation was "Katori Unit." The participants in the Leyte drop, including the regimental commander Major Shirai Gakushun, were completely wiped out. At the end of the war, only a few survivors remained on Negros Island and Luzon. The regiment's water tower still exists as a relic within the grounds of the {{ill|National Miyazaki Hospital|ja|国立病院機構宮崎病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Miyazaki Hospital|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Miyazaki Hospital|1=NHO Miyazaki National Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65255124}}.<ref>{{Cite news|title=War Heritage [1] Water Tower (Kawanami): Traces of the "Military City" Remain / Paratrooper Units Were Constantly Training|date=2014-8-11|newspaper=Miyazaki Nichinichi Shimbun|agency=Miyazaki Nichinichi Shimbun|url=http://www.the-miyanichi.co.jp/tokushu/_7461.html|accessdate=2019-12-19|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20191218171551/http://www.the-miyanichi.co.jp/tokushu/_7461.html|archivedate=2019-12-19|qq=}}</ref> * '''4th Teishin Regiment''' ('''4Ri''') - Known as the Instructional Teishin 4th Regiment at certain times. Its code name during the Leyte Operation was "Kashima Unit." At the end of the war, 24 survivors were scattered across Cebu, Mindanao, and Negros islands. The regimental commander during the Leyte Operation, Major Saita Jisaku, died in battle on Mindanao on June 20, 1945, alongside Major General Tomochika Yoshiharu, the Chief of Staff of the 35th Army. * '''5th Teishin Regiment''' ('''5Ri''') - Formed in September 1943. Initially included an infantry battalion and a heavy weapons battalion. Disbanded in November 1944, with units reorganized into the 1st and 2nd Glider Infantry Regiments. * '''1st Glider Infantry Regiment''' ('''1Ki''') - The main force was lost at sea while advancing to Luzon Island. Two independent companies, as part of the 1st Teishin Group, were nearly annihilated in the Battle of Luzon. * '''2nd Glider Infantry Regiment''' ('''2Ki''') - Nearly annihilated in the Battle of Luzon. Only about 100 survivors remained within the main force of the 1st Teishin Group in the Clark area. == Films, Military Songs, and Paintings == In 1942, amidst the fervor over the "Divine Soldiers of the Sky," the documentary film '''{{ill|Divine Soldiers of the Sky: Record of the Army Paratrooper Unit Training|ja|空の神兵#映画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Soldiers of the Sky: Record of the Army Paratrooper Unit Training|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Soldiers of the Sky: Record of the Army Paratrooper Unit Training|1=Divine Soldiers of the Sky|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597341}}''' was released, documenting the training of volunteers becoming full-fledged Teishin soldiers. The film's theme song, '''{{ill|Divine Soldiers of the Sky|ja|空の神兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Soldiers of the Sky|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Soldiers of the Sky|qq=}}''' (lyrics by Umeki Saburō, music by Takagi Tōroku), became a major hit as a military song (gunka) celebrating the paratrooper units, especially the Teishin units, and is still performed by the {{ill|1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)|en|1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)|qid=Q4596101|lt=1st Airborne Brigade (Japan Ground Self-Defense Force)|1=1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)}}, the de facto successor of these units. Numerous war paintings were created in the same year, led by Tsuruya Gorō's '''Divine Soldiers Descending on Palembang'''. Other military songs include '''Song of the Army Paratrooper Unit''' (lyrics by the Army Aviation Headquarters, music by Yamada Kōsaku) and '''Song of the Army in the Greater East Asia War''' (selected by Asahi Shimbun, lyrics by Satō Sōnosuke, music by Koseki Yūji). Notably, the 1944 semi-documentary film '''{{ill|Kato's Falcon Fighter Squadron|ja|加藤隼戦闘隊#映画『加藤隼戦闘隊』|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kato's Falcon Fighter Squadron|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kato's Falcon Fighter Squadron|1=Katō Hayabusa Sentōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q114797111}}''', depicting the 64th Flying Regiment led by Lieutenant Colonel Kato Tateo, included scenes of the Palembang Airborne Operation filmed with the cooperation of the Instructional Teishin Regiment and Flight Squadron. Postwar films depicting the Teishin units include '''{{ill|Palembang Surprise Attack Operation|ja|パレンバン奇襲作戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Palembang Surprise Attack Operation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Palembang Surprise Attack Operation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (1963) and '''{{ill|Ah! Army Falcon Fighter Squadron|ja|あゝ陸軍隼戦闘隊|1=The Falcon Fighters|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ah! Army Falcon Fighter Squadron|13=User:Immanuelle/The Falcon Fighters|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ah! Army Falcon Fighter Squadron|13=User:Immanuelle/The Falcon Fighters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q120334989}}''' (1969). The war painting '''The Takachiho Airborne Unit Attacking Enemy Airfields in Leyte''' by Japanese painter Yoshioka Kenji, created in 1945, was confiscated by GHQ as militaristic after the war but returned from the United States in 1970 as part of an indefinite loan and is now housed in the {{ill|National Museum of Modern Art, Tokyo|en|National Museum of Modern Art, Tokyo|qid=Q1359908|lt=National Museum of Modern Art, Tokyo|1=National Museum of Modern Art, Tokyo}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://search.artmuseums.go.jp/records.php?sakuhin=11338|title=Yoshioka Kenji (1906–1990) Artwork Details|publisher=Independent Administrative Institution National Museum of Art|accessdate=2022-09-02|qq=}}</ref><ref>War Paintings Open to the Public After 25 Years, ''Asahi Shimbun'', June 16, 1970, Evening Edition.</ref> <gallery> Airborne Scene - Kato hayabusa sento-tai 1944 (01) wmplayer 2013-07-17.jpg|Training with Type 97 transport aircraft (from the film "Kato's Falcon Fighter Squadron") Airborne Scene - Kato hayabusa sento-tai 1944 (02) wmplayer 2013-07-17.jpg|Teishin soldiers jumping from Type 97 transport aircraft Airborne Scene - Kato hayabusa sento-tai 1944 (03) wmplayer 2013-07-17.jpg|Same as above Airborne Scene - Kato hayabusa sento-tai 1944 (04) wmplayer 2013-07-17.jpg|Same as above Airborne Scene - Kato hayabusa sento-tai 1944 (05) wmplayer 2013-07-17.jpg|Same as above </gallery> == Memorials == In 1944, the '''{{ill|Teishin Shrine|ja|挺進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishin Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=川南護国神社|1=Kawaminami Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q79044895}}''' was established within the grounds of the Army Teishin Training Unit in Kawanami, Miyazaki Prefecture, to honor fallen soldiers. It was destroyed by GHQ after the war.<ref name=":0" /> In 1949, it was enshrined at the newly established {{ill|Kawaminami Gokoku Shrine|lt=Kawaminami Gokoku Shrine|lt=Kawanami Gokoku Shrine|ja|川南護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanami Gokoku Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanami Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name=":0" /> In 1971, the '''Statue of the Divine Soldiers of the Sky''' was erected within the {{ill|Narashino Camp|ja|習志野駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashino Camp|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashino Camp|1=Camp Narashino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610513}}, home of the 1st Airborne Brigade, by the All-Japan Airborne Comrades Association. In 1963, the '''Monument to the Birthplace of the Airborne Paratrooper Unit''' was erected within the grounds of Kawanami Gokoku Shrine. In 1991, a monument commemorating the enshrinement of over 10,000 airborne unit war dead was erected by the Airborne Veterans' Association, All-Japan Airborne Comrades Association, and bereaved families.<ref name=":0" /> Additionally, the site of the former 1st Teishin Flying Regiment Headquarters at {{ill|Nyutabaru Air Base|en|Nyutabaru Air Base|qid=Q3015913|lt=Nyutabaru Air Base|1=Nyutabaru Air Base}} features the '''Airborne Song Monument'''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://miyazaki-sensokiroku.jp/modules/addon_html6/index.php/10/index.html|title=Birthplace Monument of the Airborne Paratrooper Unit, Kawanami Town|publisher=Miyazaki War Records Preservation Center|accessdate=2019-12-19|qq=}}</ref> == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * 全日本空挺同志会 編 『空挺隊員 園田直』 全日本空挺同志会、1984th year * [[田中賢一 (軍人)|田中賢一]] 『高千穂降下部隊』 原書房、1975th year * 同上 『陸軍落下傘部隊戦記 あゝ純白の花負いて』 学陽書房、1976th year * 同上 『大空の華 空挺部隊全史』 芙蓉書房、1984th year * 徳永悦太郎 「日本陸海軍空挺部隊かく戦えり」『[[丸 (雑誌)|丸]]』エキストラ版41集、潮書房、1975th year == Related Articles == * {{ill|The Paratroopers of the Sky|ja|空の神兵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Paratroopers of the Sky|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Paratroopers of the Sky|1=Divine Soldiers of the Sky|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597341}} * {{ill|Japanese marine paratroopers of World War II|en|Japanese marine paratroopers of World War II|qid=Q4101904|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy Paratroopers|1=Japanese marine paratroopers of World War II}} - A paratrooper unit formed as part of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|日本海軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大日本帝国海軍|1=Imperial Japanese Navy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q219712}}'s {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy land forces|en|Imperial Japanese Navy land forces|qid=Q738438|lt=landing force|1=Imperial Japanese Navy land forces}}. * {{ill|1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)|en|1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)|qid=Q4596101|lt=1st Airborne Brigade|1=1st Airborne Brigade (Japan)}} - The airborne unit of the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|qid=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}}. * {{ill|Sunao Sonoda|en|Sunao Sonoda|qid=Q4388117|lt=Tadashi Sonoda|1=Sunao Sonoda}}

[[ja:挺進連隊]] [[ja:挺進部隊]] [[ja:控運部隊]]
